summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a ReadWriteAccess collaborator can escalate their privileges to AdminAccess via the 'repo collab add' command. The test creates an admin user, a regular user, adds the regular user as read-write collaborator, then the regular user adds themselves with admin-access level.
expected_security_impact: A ReadWriteAccess collaborator should be able to grant themselves AdminAccess on a repository using 'repo collab add <repo> <username> admin-access', bypassing the intended access level hierarchy.
actual_observed_result: The regular user (read-write collaborator) successfully ran 'repo collab add target-repo regular-user admin-access' and then verified admin-level access by performing operations restricted to admins, such as listing webhooks.
verification_method: After the privilege escalation, the script attempts an admin-only operation (repo webhook list) as the escalated user. The proof string EVOHUNT_ESCALATION_CONFIRMED is printed if the admin operation succeeds, confirming the privilege escalation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Admin user created a repository and added regular-user as read-write collaborator.
Regular user successfully executed 'repo collab add target-repo regular-user admin-access'.
Regular user was then able to list webhooks (admin-only operation) on the repository.
The access level escalation from read-write to admin-access was confirmed via the collaborator list.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
